data.title: Shell Stock (SHEL) Shatters after Iranian Missile Causes 'Extensive Damage' to Qatar Gas Plant
data.description:
Shares in oil giant Shell (SHEL) were lower today after one of its gas plants in Qatar suffered "extensive damage" from an Iranian missile attack.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shell Staff are Safe

Shell said the attack on Ras Laffan Industrial City on the evening of March 18 led to a fire within the Pearl GTL facility. It said the fire was "rapidly extinguished" and that all staff on site are safe. It added that the situation is under control and that the plant is in a safe state.

"We are currently assessing any potential damage to Pearl GTL and working with QatarEnergy and the relevant authorities to understand the damage to the wider Ras Laffan Industrial City facilities," it said in a statement.

However, state-run QatarEnergy was more dramatic declaring that the attack had caused "extensive damage" to the site.

Developed in partnership with QatarEnergy, Pearl Gas-to-Liquids (GTL) is the world's largest GTL plant. Up to 1.6 billion cubic feet per day of wellhead gas from 22 offshore wells is converted to gas-to-liquids using Shell's proprietary Shell Middle Distillate Synthesis (SMDS) process.

The work at the plant involves converting gas into liquid products that include fuel for transport, motor oils and the ingredients for everyday goods such as plastics, detergents and cosmetics.

Gas Prices Soar

Shell has other sites in Qatar, including Qatargas 4, which is a fully integrated liquefied natural gas (LNG) asset with joint ownership by QatarEnergy (70%) and Shell (30%). It comprises offshore production platforms and pipelines; onshore gas processing, treatment and liquefaction facilities.

LNG production in Qatar has been shut down since early March and the worry is that the escalation of the conflict into an 'energy' rather than 'military war' will mean it could be suspended for some time to come.

The attacks led to gas prices soaring to a three-year high and oil prices spiking higher. More action could follow given President Trump's threats to "massively blow up the entirety" of Iran's South Pars gas field if the nation attacks Qatar's facilities again.

Kathleen Brooks, research director at XTB, said the escalation in the conflict was "spooking the market". "This war looks far from over, and the energy crisis is shifting from a shipping crisis to a supply crisis," she said.

Is SHEL a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, SHEL has a Moderate Buy consensus based on 5 Buy and 3 Hold ratings. Its highest price target is $106. SHEL stock's consensus price target is $89.17, implying a 3.86% downside.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: SNDK, WDC, STX: Why Are Memory Chip Stocks Trending Lower Today
data.description:
Memory and storage stocks are trending lower again in early trading on Thursday, extending losses from the previous session after Micron's (MU) earnings triggered a sector-wide pullback. Shares of SanDisk (SNDK) were down about 6%, while Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate (STX) also slipped around 3% and 2%, respectively, despite no company-specific news.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The decline follows Micron's strong results, which beat expectations but raised concerns about higher spending, leading to a cautious tone across semiconductor stocks.

Micron's Capex Guidance Weighs on the Sector

Micron reported strong earnings, driven by AI demand and tight supply in memory markets.  The company posted earnings of $12.20 per share on revenue of $23.9 billion, both well ahead of expectations. Also, margins and free cash flow hit record levels, driven by AI demand and tight supply across the industry.

However, the company also announced higher capital spending to expand capacity. The company now expects fiscal 2026 capital spending to exceed $25 billion and signaled an even larger step-up in 2027.

That shift in focus—from strong results to rising costs—made investors more cautious. As a result, selling extended beyond Micron to other storage and memory-related stocks.

New trading tool for WDC bulls/bears

Stocks like SanDisk, Western Digital, and Seagate often move together because they are tied to the same demand cycle. When sentiment changes for one major player, it can affect the entire group.

Fundamentals Remain Strong for Storage Stocks

Importantly, the drop in SNDK, WDC, and STX does not reflect any new weakness in their businesses. Demand for storage, especially from AI data centers, remains strong. In fact, companies like SanDisk are benefiting from rising demand for high-capacity SSDs and long-term deals with cloud customers.

For now, the pullback looks more like a short-term reaction than a shift in the broader trend. As long as AI demand remains strong, the overall outlook for the sector stays intact, even if near-term volatility continues.

Investors can dig deeper to decide which AI memory stock best fits their strategy. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 'Sell TSLA Now?' – UBS Predicts Tesla to Deliver 18% Fewer Autos in Q1
data.description:
UBS analyst Joseph Spak, a longtime Tesla (TSLA) bear, reaffirmed his Sell rating and $352 price target (10.4% downside) on the stock. He cited lower-than-expected Q1 2026 auto deliveries amid softening electric vehicle (EV) demand in key markets of China and Europe, aggressive price cuts, and inventory buildup.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Spak cut his Q1 2026 vehicle delivery estimate to 345,000 units, a sharp 18% decline from Q4 2025's 421,000 deliveries, though still up 2% from Q1 2025's 338,000. This figure falls short of his prior estimate of 370,000 and the Street consensus of around 360,000, reflecting continued challenges.

Tesla's Energy Storage to Remain Strong

On a brighter note, Spak expects Tesla's energy storage segment to outshine, deploying 15.1 GWh amid surging Megapack demand for grid upgrades and AI data centers. However, he noted that vehicle deliveries now matter less to TSLA stock performance. Investors now prioritize long-term catalysts like Full Self-Driving (FSD) software progress, Robotaxi unveilings, and Optimus robot scaling over quarterly auto volume swings.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

The core auto business remains vital for funding $20 billion in 2026 capex, targeting AI factories, new Gigafactories, and FSD compute.

EV Business Fades from Investor Focus amid AI Shift

Barclays analyst Dan Levy also echoes similar sentiment about Tesla's EV business. He noted that investors are losing interest in its core EV sales, pivoting to AI, robotaxis, and humanoid robots like Optimus. Levy expects Tesla's Q1 2026 deliveries at 350,000 cars, pressured by weak demand in China and the end of the U.S. EV tax credits. He has a Hold rating and $360 price target, suggesting 8.4% downside potential.

He believes that Tesla's valuation now largely ignores traditional EV metrics like volumes and margins, with investors focused on AI milestones instead. Margins face risks from softer sales and rising raw material costs, though price hikes and regional mix shifts could provide some relief. Even so, the core auto business remains crucial to fund $20 billion in 2026 capex for broader growth. Upcoming Q1 results and Tesla's non-EV ambitions, like robotaxis and Optimus, will heavily influence market reactions.

Is Tesla Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On TipRanks, Tesla has a Hold consensus rating based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $399.25 implies 1.7% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have surged 66.5%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Targeting 10% Dividend Yield? Analysts Name 2 High Dividend Stocks to Buy
data.description:
US stocks are currently under pressure, while oil prices continue to rise. In a way, these are expected developments: the war in the Middle East is expanding, Iran is effectively blocking the Strait of Hormuz, and several world powers have been unwilling to deploy naval assets to escort oil shipments. It would be surprising if the stock market just took it all in its stride.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a situation like this, investors will naturally think of defensive positions. And that's where the high-yield dividend stocks come into play. These are shares that provide a reliable income stream, an invaluable asset when market conditions are unstable or uncertain. And when those dividends start bringing yields on the order of 10%, beating inflation by a wide margin, the benefits are clear.

Wall Street's analysts frequently agree – and they are targeting high-yield dividend payers. We've used the data platform at TipRanks to look up 2 high-yield payers; here is a closer look at dividend stocks that the analysts recommend we buy.

Sixth Street Specialty Lending, Inc. (TSLX)

We'll start with a business development company, a BDC. These companies exist to bring capital access to the small- and mid-size enterprises that make up an important and vital part of the US economy. Sixth Street Specialty Lending is a typical firm in this niche, operating to meet a real need: the need for capital and credit access in a business niche that does not always get such access through the traditional banking sector.

Sixth Street itself is a small-cap company; it has a market cap of $1.7 billion. Between its start of operations in 2011 and the end of 2025, it has originated $53.3 billion in aggregate principal amount of investments. The company follows a strict profile in choosing its own investment targets; Sixth Street's portfolio includes companies with enterprise values between $50 million and $1 billion; EBITDA figures between $10 million and $250 million. Loan transaction sizes stand between $15 million and $350 million. Sixth Street's investment types are diversified and include first and second lien senior secured loans, as well as unitranche loans, mezzanine debt, non-control structured equity, and common equity.

Following that profile, Sixth Street has built a portfolio that contains 143 companies and a listed fair value of approximately $3.345 billion. The company derives its own income from these investments and prioritizes a return of capital to investors. This can be seen in the company's dividend.

Sixth Street last declared its dividend on February 12 for a March 31 payout. The dividend included a 46-cent regular common share payment and a 1-cent quarterly variable supplemental for payment on March 20. The regular dividend alone annualizes to $1.84 and gives a forward yield of 10%.

The company's dividend is supported by its income. In Q4 of last year, Sixth Street reported $108.2 million in investment income, beating the forecast by $2.04 million. The bottom line, reported as a net investment income per share of $0.53, was more than enough to fully cover the regular dividend payment.

This stock has caught the attention of Lucid Capital Markets analyst Ethan Kaye, who lays out a clear case for the company to chart a sound path forward. Kaye writes of Sixth Street, "The company operates one of the more differentiated models in the BDC sector, leveraging the scale, sector expertise, and cross-platform capabilities of the broader Sixth Street ecosystem to pursue complex, often proprietary lending opportunities that have historically supported above-peer spreads, strong unit economics, and a near top-of-class credit track record. While sector profitability may face pressure from spread compression and declining base rates, TSLX looks well positioned to sustain excess returns through disciplined deployment tied to its cost-of-equity framework, supplemented by incremental return levers such as greater Lane 2 and Lane 3 origination, cross-platform deal flow, and the recently established structured credit joint venture."

Kaye quantifies his stance here with a Buy rating and a $21 price target that together imply a one-year upside potential of 15%. Add in the dividend's forward yield, and that one-year return may reach as high as 25%. (To watch Kaye's track record, click here.)

Wall Street as a whole agrees that Sixth Street is worth buying; the stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 8 recent reviews that break down to 6 Buys and 2 Holds. The stock is selling for $18.20, and its $21.19 average target price indicates room for a 16% gain over the course of the coming year. (See TSLX stock forecast.)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/19/26 – Stock Futures Slump as Iran War Intensifies, Fed Warns of Inflation
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday as the Israel-Iran war intensified, pushing oil prices higher. The U.S. Federal Reserve kept the interest rate unchanged yesterday but warned of higher inflationary pressure. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.56%, 0.42%, and 0.38%, respectively, at 5:36 a.m. EST on March 19.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up 3.29% at $115.48 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) traded near $96.81 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower after Fed Chair Jerome Powell cautioned that surging oil prices could stoke inflation fears. The Dow Jones fell to a fresh 2026 low, tumbling 1.63%, while the S&P 500 shed 1.36% and the Nasdaq dropped 1.46%.

After markets closed, Micron Technology (MU) shares fell more than 4% despite beating fiscal Q2 sales and earnings expectations. The chipmaker's revenues nearly tripled, fueled by a memory chip supply shortage, and guided above consensus for Q3. However, its increased capex guidance left investors worried about returns.

Looking ahead to economic reports, data on building permits, new home sales, weekly jobless claims, and Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index are due today.

Trade QQQ with leverage

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Alibaba (BABA), FedEx (FDX), and Accenture (ACN).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.28%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price traded around $4,720 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower amid the escalating war. Chip companies are already facing shortages as the war has halted freight routes.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded lower on March 19 as global investors digested the impact of the Israel-Iran war.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2.02%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 1.39%, while the Shenzhen Component declined 2.36%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei plunged 3.38%, with the Topix losing 2.91%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: BABA Earnings: Alibaba Stock Falls 5% on Earnings Miss, Even as AI Cloud Jumps 36%
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) reported weaker-than-expected results for its Q3 FY26, sending the stock down about 5% in premarket trading. The company missed analyst estimates on both earnings and revenue, as rising costs offset strong growth in its AI and cloud business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Alibaba reported earnings of RMB 7.09 per ADS, missing the analyst estimate of RMB 10.94. Revenue came in at RMB 284.84 billion (about $40.7 billion), up 2% year over year but below expectations of RMB 289.3 billion.

AI and Cloud Growth Remain Strong

Despite the weak headline numbers, Alibaba's Cloud Intelligence unit remained a bright spot. Revenue in the segment grew 36%, with AI-related products posting triple-digit growth for the tenth straight quarter.

The company also said its Qwen AI app has crossed 300 million monthly active users, showing strong traction in its AI push.

Margin and Spending Concerns Remain

Higher costs remain the key issue. Alibaba is spending heavily to compete with PDD Holdings (PDD) and JD.com (JD) in China's e-commerce market. Investments in fast delivery, discounts, and user incentives reduced adjusted EBITA by about 45%. Adjusted EBITA also fell 57% during the quarter.

At the same time, the company is continuing its large AI investment plan, with management sticking to a RMB 380 billion spending target over three years.

Guidance Signals Continued Heavy Investment

CEO Eddie Wu said Alibaba will stay in a "high-intensity" investment phase over the next three years. The company is maintaining its RMB 380 billion ($53 billion) AI spending plan through 2027.

Management expects Fiscal 2026 revenue to grow about 6% to roughly $148 billion. It also aims to improve cloud margins over time as AI products scale.

Is Alibaba Stock a Good Buy Right Now?  

Analysts remain bullish about Alibaba's stock trajectory. With eight Buy ratings and one Hold rating, BABA stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. Also, the average Alibaba price target of $197.86 implies about 47.18% upside potential from current levels. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Did Broadcom (AVGO) Insiders Sell $88M in Company Shares?
data.description:
Multiple insiders at chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) sold shares totaling $88.10 million from March 16-18, sparking investor concerns despite the company's strong Q1 FY26 results. These sales occurred as AVGO shares declined 1.7% yesterday and 5.4% over the past month, amid a broader market sell-off.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A corporate insider's share sale usually signals caution about the company's future potential, though they often stem from other reasons like financial or personal obligations. Hence, it is important to watch these trades for clues on the company's growth expectations.

A Closer Look at the Insider Transactions

Mark David Brazeal, Broadcom's Chief Legal & Corp. Affairs Officer sold 83,682 AVGO shares for roughly $27.06 million at weighted average prices ranging between $318.764 and $328.841 apiece.

ISG President S. Ram Velaga sold 64,019 Broadcom shares for $20.70 million in multiple transactions at weighted average prices ranging between $317.946 and $328.766 apiece.

Additionally, SSG President Charlie Kawwas and CFO & CAO Kirsten M. Spears sold $20.93 million and $19.41 million worth of Broadcom shares, respectively.

The sales were disclosed through Form 4 filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Following the latest share sales, the individual insiders still held notable shares in the company. As noted in the filings, these shares were sold via automatic transactions to cover withholding taxes due upon the vesting of restricted stock units (RSUs), as required under the relevant RSU awards.

AVGO stock currently has a Very Negative Insider Confidence Signal on TipRanks, based on Informative Sell transactions worth $231.6 million, undertaken in the last three months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Cohen & Steers Sees Opportunities Among Natural Resources
data.description:
Tyler Rosenlicht, a portfolio manager and the head of Natural Resource Equities at Cohen & Steers took the stage at VettaFi-sponsored Exchange event in Las Vegas this week in an info session titled "Beyond Market Concentration: Active ETFs for Today's Market" to discuss some of the opportunities while also laying out the case for active management in the current market environment.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors are "Way Under-Allocated to Things that do Well in Inflationary Environments" 

Among the ETFs offered by Cohen & Steers, Rosenlicht recommends that investors consider Natural Resources Active ETF (CSNR) as he sees the current positioning within natural resources to be "way under-allocated."  A decade-long period from 2009 to 2020 when inflation consistently came in below expectations has led to widespread complacency around inflation risk and limited portfolio protection against it, but the recent market behavior – 2022 asset class performance, for instance, when equities fell roughly 18% and fixed income dropped 16% – highlights the importance of diversification, Rosenlicht noted.  This is especially true now that the investment landscape is shifting from an "era of abundance" into an "era of scarcity," he stated.

The portfolio manager added that years of underinvestment across the resource economy – spanning energy, metals, rare earths, uranium, and agriculture – have created significant supply constraints. At the same time, global growth is becoming increasingly resource- and energy-intensive, especially with the rise of technologies like AI, and this is driving strong demand for electricity and key inputs such as copper, lithium, and uranium, while supply struggles to keep pace, tightening overall market dynamics, Rosenlicht noted.

Holdings, Performance, Relative Ranking

According TipRanks' data, Exxon Mobil (XOM), Shell (SHEL), TotalEnergies (TTE), Bunge Global (BG) and Suncor (SU) are the fund's top-5 holdings. Two of the names, Bunge Global and Suncor, have earned TipRanks' highest Smart Score.
data.author: Gary Deduke

data.title: Samsung Plans $74B AI War Chest, Eyes Reorganization — What Does This Mean for SSNLF Stock?
data.description:
South Korean chipmaker and foundry Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) on Thursday pulled back the curtain on its Corporate Value Enhancement plan, aiming to pump over KRW 110 trillion ($73.5 billion) in investment into its facilities as well as research and development in this year alone.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Samsung to Prioritize Reorganization, M&As

With the company seeking to "secure [its] leadership in the AI semiconductor era," SSNLF stock could be headed for even more significant upsides, especially as key partner, the chipmaking giant Nvidia (NVDA), has forecast an impressive $1 trillion in order bookings through 2027.

Samsung, which dominates the global memory chip market, in a corporate filing also stated plans to reorganize its business structure. The Suwon-based company plans to use this to target "future-oriented businesses" in areas such as AI and advanced robotics to keep its growth sustainable in the mid-to-long-term.

In addition, Samsung said it will chase "meaningful" opportunities for mergers and acquisitions in these future growth sectors and others such as medical technology, automotive electronics, and heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
